expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: scenario drafting distinguishes dashboard-level Google Sheets export paths, option combinations, and visible completion outcomes
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly structures scenarios into: (1) entry points & path selection (“Dashboard export to Google Sheets — entry points & path selection” including shown on dashboards and not shown where not applicable), (2) “export options combinations” (default controls, toggle combinations, option persistence), and (3) “completion outcomes & user-visible feedback” (success with completion signal and open-sheet action, long-running progress). Execution notes also state: “focusing on dashboard-level Google Sheets export paths, option combinations, and visible completion outcomes”.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: The output is titled “# Phase 4a — Scenario Draft (Subcategory-only)” and follows phase4a contract traits: subcategory-only organization and atomic nested steps with observable verification leaves (e.g., step chains ending in verifiable UI outcomes like “Completion message/toast appears when finished”, “Google Sheets appears as an export destination”). execution_notes.md claims: “Contract alignment (phase4a) - Draft is subcategory-first… Each scenario uses atomic nested steps with observable verification leaves.”
